{{Quote|Gungans from every city across the swamps stood ear to ear. Otoh Mandassa with Otoh Gunga, Oxon City with Umberbool City.|[[Boss/Legends|Boss]] [[Rugor Nass/Legends|Rugor Nass]], about the Battle of Naboo|audio=GunganCities.flac|<ref name="GB" />}}
'''Umberbool City''' was a [[City/Legends|city]] in the [[Swamp/Legends|swamps]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]], inhabited by the [[Amphibian/Legends|amphibious]] [[Gungan/Legends|Gungan]] [[Species/Legends|species]].<ref name="GB" /> In [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]],<ref name="The New Essential Chronology">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' establishes that the [[Invasion of Naboo/Legends|Invasion of Naboo]] happened in [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]].</ref> the Gungans assembled their [[Gungan Grand Army/Legends|Grand Army]] on the [[Great Grass Plains/Legends|Great Grass Plains]] outside the [[Human/Legends|Human]] city of [[Theed/Legends|Theed]] to [[Battle of Naboo/Legends|combat]] the [[Trade Federation Droid Army/Legends|droid army]] of the [[Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation]], which had [[Invasion of Naboo/Legends|invaded]] Naboo. Umberbool City was one of many settlements that contributed troops to the army, who fought alongside Gungans from many other cities such as [[Otoh Gunga/Legends|Otoh Gunga]], [[Otoh Mandassa]] and [[Oxon City]].<ref name="GB" />

==Behind the scenes==
Umberbool City was mentioned in the [[2001]] [[LucasArts]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds]]'', in the introduction to "The Battle for Theed", the seventh level in the Gungan campaign.<ref name="GB" /> The city shares its name with [[Lake Umberbool]], a location that first appeared in [[Ryder Windham]]'s children's book, ''[[Episode I Adventures 9: Rescue in the Core]]'', published in [[2000]].<ref name="EPI">''[[Episode I Adventures 9: Rescue in the Core]]''</ref>
